title: centrapalus subaphyllus
intro: Centrapalus subaphyllus, also known as the Cape ragwort or Stinking Mayweed, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae, native to southern Africa. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, Centrapalus subaphyllus is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be used in topical creams or ointments to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
